query: NWS Climate Prediction Center June 2026 precipitation outlook Gulf Coast Texas
answer:
This is excellent — the sub-agent was able to retrieve and analyze the actual CPC prognostic discussions. Let me compile the full research report.

---

## NWS Climate Prediction Center June 2026 Precipitation Outlook: Gulf Coast Texas

### Key Finding

The **CPC 30-day outlook for June 2026** (issued May 31, 2026, by forecaster Dan Collins) **favors above-normal precipitation for Gulf Coast Texas**. However, the broader **JJA (June-July-August) seasonal outlook** (issued May 21, 2026, by Brad Pugh) leans **slightly toward below-normal precipitation along the western Gulf Coast**, including the Texas coastline. This creates a split signal: a wetter-than-normal June followed by an expected drying trend later in the summer.

---

### 30-Day (June 2026) Outlook

- **Forecast category:** Above-normal precipitation favored for Gulf Coast states, including Texas
- **Probability range:** Typically 33–50% for the above-normal category (CPC standard tilt language)
- **Basis:** CFSv2 precipitation forecast for June and ECMWF forecasts for the first half of the month
- **Issued:** May 31, 2026

### Seasonal (JJA 2026) Outlook

- **Forecast category:** Slightly below-normal precipitation along the western Gulf Coast
- **Probability range:** Approximately 33–40% for the below-normal category
- **Basis:** El Niño composites, dynamical model output, statistical tools
- **Issued:** May 21, 2026
- **Key note:** The dry signal in El Niño composites peaks in JAS (July-August-September) for the south-central CONUS, explaining the divergence between the wetter June outlook and drier seasonal outlook.

### ENSO Context

- **Current status:** ENSO-neutral, transitioning to El Niño (El Niño Watch issued)
- **Niño-3.4 index:** +0.4 to +0.5°C (near the El Niño threshold)
- **El Niño probability:** 82% for May-June-July 2026; 96% by Dec 2026–Feb 2027
- **Strength potential:** ~67% chance of strong El Niño (Niño-3.4 ≥ +1.5°C) by Oct-Nov-Dec 2026
- **Subsurface anomalies:** Positive anomalies exceeding +2°C at 100–150m depth near the Date Line, increasing for 6 consecutive months

### Additional Forecast Drivers

- **MJO:** Convective phase propagated across the Maritime Continent and amplified over the Western Pacific; models predict deamplification as it moves eastward
- **Soil moisture:** Considered as a factor in the seasonal outlook
- **SSTs:** Coastal sea surface temperatures and the emerging El Niño warming pattern are key drivers
- **Models used:** CFSv2, ECMWF, NMME, IMME, CCA, Constructed Analog, ENSO-OCN

### Climatological Context

- June is typically one of the **wettest months** for Gulf Coast Texas (Houston/Galveston area: ~5–6 inches normal)
- Precipitation is highly variable, often dominated by a small number of heavy rain events
- Historically, **El Niño summers produce drier-than-normal conditions** for the south-central US, but this influence is weaker in early summer (June) and strengthens in JAS

### Summary Table

| Timeframe | Forecast for Gulf Coast TX | Primary Basis |
|---|---|---|
| **June 2026 (30-day)** | Above normal favored | CFSv2, ECMWF first-half forecasts |
| **JJA 2026 (seasonal)** | Slightly below normal (western Gulf Coast) | El Niño composites, dynamical models |

---

sources.title: CPC 30-Day (Monthly) Prognostic Discussion - June 2026
sources.snippet: Above normal precipitation is favored for most of the Gulf Coast states and the Southeast Atlantic coast, consistent with the CFSv2 precipitation forecast for June and ECMWF forecasts for the first half of the month.

sources.title: CPC Long-Lead Seasonal (90-Day) Prognostic Discussion - JJA 2026
sources.snippet: The JJA outlook leans slightly towards below-normal precipitation along the western Gulf Coast based on composites and dynamical model output. A dry signal in El Nino composites peaks in JAS throughout the south-central CONUS.

sources.title: CPC ENSO Diagnostic Discussion
sources.snippet: ENSO-neutral conditions continued but El Nino Watch issued. 82% chance of El Nino in MJJ 2026. Positive subsurface anomalies exceeding +2C at 100-150m depth. Near 2 in 3 chance of strong El Nino by OND.

sources.title: CPC 30-Day Outlook Main Page
sources.snippet: Official 30-day outlook page with precipitation and temperature maps for June 2026.

key_facts: CPC 30-day outlook for June 2026 (issued May 31, 2026) favors above-normal precipitation for Gulf Coast states including Texas, based on CFSv2 and ECMWF forecasts.
CPC JJA 2026 seasonal outlook (issued May 21, 2026) leans slightly toward below-normal precipitation along the western Gulf Coast (including Texas coastline), based on El Niño composites.
ENSO-neutral conditions are transitioning to El Niño; El Niño Watch has been issued with 82% probability of El Niño in May-June-July 2026.
Niño-3.4 index at +0.4 to +0.5°C, near the El Niño threshold; subsurface anomalies exceeding +2°C at 100-150m depth.
Approximately 67% chance of strong El Niño (Niño-3.4 ≥ +1.5°C) by Oct-Nov-Dec 2026; 96% chance El Niño continues through winter 2026-27.
The dry signal in El Niño composites peaks in JAS (July-Aug-Sep) for south-central CONUS, explaining why June is wetter but the full summer leans drier.
Normal June precipitation for Houston/Galveston Gulf Coast area is approximately 5-6 inches.
MJO convective phase amplified over Western Pacific, interacting with emerging El Niño; models predict deamplification.
Split signal: wetter-than-normal June expected, followed by drying trend as El Niño influence strengthens later in summer.
Forecast models used include CFSv2, ECMWF, NMME, IMME, CCA, Constructed Analog, and ENSO-OCN.
